meta.subject: Mathematics
notation: Use the 'therefore' symbol (∴) before final answers
Use decimal point (not comma) for decimals
Coordinates written as (x; y) with semicolon
markingStandards: Full marks require all working shown
meta.assessmentFormat: Paper 1: Numbers, Operations and Relationships; Patterns, Functions and Algebra. Paper 2: Space and Shape (Geometry), Measurement, Data Handling (including Probability).

subtopics.name: Whole Numbers
skills: Properties of whole numbers (commutative, associative, distributive)
Calculations using all four operations with whole numbers, estimation and rounding
Multiples, factors, prime factors, LCM and HCF (by inspection or prime factorisation)
Solve problems involving ratio, rate and financial contexts
methods: Use estimation and rounding strategies
Long multiplication and division in columns
Prime factorisation for HCF and LCM
commonSteps: Use prime factors for multiples and factors

subtopics.name: Integers
skills: Add, subtract, multiply and divide integers
Perform calculations involving squares, cubes, square roots and cube roots of integers
Apply order of operations (BODMAS)
Properties of integers (commutative, associative, distributive, inverses)
methods: BODMAS order of operations
Sign rules for multiplication and division
Adding a negative equals subtracting a positive
commonSteps: Work left to right for multiplication/division then addition/subtraction

subtopics.name: Common Fractions, Decimal Fractions and Percentages
skills: Add, subtract, multiply and divide common fractions (including mixed numbers and division by fractions)
Calculate squares, cubes, square roots and cube roots of fractions and decimals
Convert between common fractions, decimal fractions and percentages
Calculate percentage of an amount, percentage increase and decrease
Order and compare rational numbers
methods: Find LCD for addition and subtraction of fractions
Multiply by reciprocal for division of fractions
Percentage: (part/whole) × 100 or new amount = original × (1 ± rate/100)
commonSteps: Convert to equivalent fractions with common denominator where needed
Simplify all answers to lowest terms
notation: Mixed numbers or improper fractions — follow question instruction

subtopics.name: Exponents
skills: Write repeated multiplication in exponential form and calculate powers of integers and rational numbers
Compare and represent numbers in exponential form and scientific notation (positive exponents)
Apply basic exponent laws: aᵐ × aⁿ = aᵐ⁺ⁿ, aᵐ ÷ aⁿ = aᵐ⁻ⁿ (m > n), (aᵐ)ⁿ = aᵐⁿ, (a × b)ⁿ = aⁿ × bⁿ, a⁰ = 1
Perform calculations involving exponents, squares, cubes, square roots and cube roots
Solve problems in contexts involving exponents
methods: aᵐ means a multiplied by itself m times
Same base: add exponents when multiplying, subtract when dividing
Power of a power: multiply exponents
commonSteps: Check if bases are the same for laws
notation: Write base and exponent clearly: 2³ not 23

subtopics.name: Numeric and Geometric Patterns; Functions and Relationships
skills: Investigate and extend numeric and geometric patterns (including those represented in tables, diagrams or algebraically)
Describe rules or relationships in own words or algebraic language
Determine input and output values or rules using flow diagrams, tables, formulae and equations
Interpret and justify equivalence of different descriptions of the same relationship
methods: Look for constant difference or constant ratio
Describe general rules
commonSteps: Represent in different forms (table, diagram, equation)

subtopics.name: Algebraic Expressions
skills: Recognise conventions for writing algebraic expressions
Identify coefficients, variables, constants, like and unlike terms
Simplify by collecting like terms
Multiply a monomial by a binomial or trinomial (expand and simplify)
Determine squares, cubes, square roots and cube roots of algebraic terms
methods: Like terms have the same variables with the same exponents
Distributive law: a(b + c) = ab + ac
commonSteps: Distribute the monomial to each term in the bracket

subtopics.name: Algebraic Equations
skills: Solve one-step and two-step linear equations
Solve equations with the variable on one or both sides (simple cases)
Set up and solve simple equations from word problems
methods: Use inverse operations
Balance method: perform the same operation on both sides
commonSteps: Identify the operation applied to the variable
Apply the inverse to both sides

subtopics.name: Constructions, Geometry of Straight Lines and 2D Shapes
skills: Perform basic constructions of geometric figures
Apply angle relationships: angles on a straight line (180°), angles at a point (360°), vertically opposite angles equal, perpendicular lines
Classify triangles by sides and angles; apply angle sum of triangle (180°)
Classify quadrilaterals; apply angle sum of quadrilateral (360°)
Identify properties of 2D shapes
methods: Angles on a straight line sum to 180°
Angles at a point sum to 360°
Vertically opposite angles are equal
commonSteps: Identify the angle relationship from the diagram
Solve for the unknown with reason

subtopics.name: Theorem of Pythagoras
skills: Identify the hypotenuse in a right-angled triangle
Use a² + b² = c² to find an unknown side
Use the converse to determine if a triangle is right-angled
methods: c is always the longest side (hypotenuse)
Converse: if a² + b² = c² then the triangle is right-angled at the vertex opposite c
commonSteps: Identify the right angle and hypotenuse
Label sides a, b, c (c = hypotenuse)
Solve and take square root if needed
notation: Leave answer in surd form unless instructed otherwise

subtopics.name: Area and Perimeter of 2D Shapes
skills: Calculate perimeter of polygons and circles
Calculate area of rectangles, triangles, circles and trapeziums
Calculate area and perimeter of composite and irregular 2D shapes
methods: Break composite shapes into basic shapes
Add or subtract areas as appropriate
commonSteps: Substitute values (use π ≈ 3.14 or as instructed)
notation: Round to 2 decimal places or as instructed
keyFormulae: Rectangle: A = l × b, P = 2(l + b)

subtopics.name: Surface Area and Volume of 3D Objects
skills: Calculate volume of prisms (including rectangular and triangular) and cylinders
Calculate surface area of prisms and cylinders
Solve problems involving volume and surface area of 3D objects
methods: Volume of prism = area of base × height
Surface area = sum of areas of all faces
commonSteps: Identify the 3D shape and its components
keyFormulae: Rectangular prism: V = l × b × h, SA = 2(lb + lh + bh)

subtopics.name: Collecting, Organising, Summarising and Representing Data
skills: Organise data using frequency tables and tallies
Draw bar graphs and histograms
Calculate mean, median and mode
Read, interpret, analyse and report on data and graphs
methods: Mean = sum of values / number of values
Median = middle value in ordered data
Mode = most frequent value
commonSteps: Organise and sort data if needed
Label graphs correctly with title and scale

subtopics.name: Probability
skills: Understand probability as a value between 0 and 1 measuring chance
List all possible outcomes in a sample space (using tables or tree diagrams)
Calculate probability of a single event
Understand relative frequency and its relation to theoretical probability
methods: P(event) = number of favourable outcomes / total number of outcomes
commonSteps: List the complete sample space systematically
Divide and express as a fraction in simplest form
notation: P(A) for probability of event A
Probability expressed as a fraction between 0 and 1
